name: autoreview
tagline: Runs a bundled multi-engine (Codex/Claude/Pi) code review helper as a pre-commit/pre-ship closeout gate, with scope-freeze and release.
verdict: Thorough, well-engineered review-gating skill: strong isolation guidance for reviewer subprocesses, explicit failure handling (oversized bundles, Sol->Terra fallback, gitcrawl repair), a defined output contract, and clean re-run semantics. Weakest spots are hygiene: a 34KB single file that could split reference tables out, no version/changelog signal.

highlights.summary: Terse one-line description ('Codex default; optional Claude or Pi') undersells opt-in capabilities like credential-staged parallel test delegation and release-branch scope gating.
442-line/34KB single file inlines large reference material (models/thinking tables, env-var tables, isolation-flags table) with obvious split points into reference files.
442 lines (under the 500-line soft cap) but 34KB, well over the 25KB soft budget.
